title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Poirot reveals that Jenny and Nancy Drew conspired to manipulate Richard Nigges's suicide pact. Originally, Nigges intended for himself, Harriet, Ada, and Jenny to die, with Nigges killing the others before committing suicide. Following Nancy's advice, Jenny convinced Nigges that she should be the one to kill him instead. To achieve this, Jenny lied to Nigges, claiming Nancy was evil and had plotted to ruin Frances.

Jenny and Nancy also enlisted Samuel Kid to assist with their plan. To avoid the danger of climbing out of a window in Room 238, Samuel helped them exit and impersonated Nigges. While Nigges believed Jenny would survive to frame Nancy for a murder at the hotel, Nancy had already prepared an alibi with the Wallace couple. Jenny explains her motive was survival, as she believed Nigges would kill her if she opposed him. She hoped the plan would result in only a short prison sentence for her and Nancy, after which she intended to marry Samuel. Although Poirot accuses Jenny of killing Harriet Sipper and Richard Nigges, Jenny claims she killed Nigges in self-defense.

Four days later, Poirot visits Caderpillar at Mrs. Blanche Onsworth's house. They discuss the Baskerville Hotel case and Poirot's methods. Poirot admits he deliberately misled Caderpillar regarding Jeanne Hobby's death and the bloodstains in Room 402 to force him to use his "grey cells." Poirot explains the bloodstains were a deception designed to make investigators believe a body had been dragged, whereas a killer would have used a container like a suitcase or laundry cart. The conversation concludes with Poirot presenting Caderpillar with a letter from Dr. Ambrose Flavard and his wife.
